ogTitle: Lake St Clair vs Lanena: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake St Clair and Lanena.
heading: Lake St Clair vs Lanena — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake St Clair, Tasmania and Lanena, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lanena has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Lake St Clair (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake St Clair or Lanena safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake St Clair has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Lanena scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake St Clair scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Lanena scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake St Clair or Lanena?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lanena has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.67% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
